perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Supplier will receive Purchase Orders by telephone, USPS, overnight courier, email, and fax transmission, Monday through Friday except on state or nationally recognized bank holidays. Purchase Orders not received by 3:00 p.m. Eastern Time are considered to be received on the following Page 1 business day. Supplier will confirm or reject Purchase Orders within [***] of receipt of the Purchase Order. Orders not rejected in writing within such time will be deemed confirmed and accepted by Supplier. Each Purchase Order issued by PNC or its TPMs and accepted by Supplier shall be governed by the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Additional terms included in acknowledgments, standard terms and conditions, or any other documents or communications exchanged by the Parties in connection with the sale or purchase of any Ingredients shall be void and of no force or effect. The Parties may only modify, add to or amend any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement by a writing signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Supplier will receive Purchase Orders by telephone, USPS, overnight courier, email, and fax transmission, Monday through Friday except on state or nationally recognized bank holidays. Purchase Orders not received by 3:00 p.m. Eastern Time are considered to be received on the following Page 1 business day. Supplier will confirm or reject Purchase Orders within [***] of receipt of the Purchase Order. Orders not rejected in writing within such time will be deemed confirmed and accepted by Supplier, unless the supplier notifies PNC verbally about rejection within 1 hour of reciept. Each Purchase Order issued by PNC or its TPMs and accepted by Supplier shall be governed by the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Additional terms included in acknowledgments, standard terms and conditions, or any other documents or communications exchanged by the Parties in connection with the sale or purchase of any Ingredients shall be void and of no force or effect. The Parties may only modify, add to or amend any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement by a writing signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'unless the supplier notifies PNC verbally about rejection within 1 hour of receipt' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that orders not rejected in writing within a specified time are deemed confirmed. The added clause allows for verbal rejection within 1 hour, conflicting with the written rejection requirement. This introduces uncertainty about the valid method and timeframe for rejecting purchase orders.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 Supplier will receive Purchase Orders by telephone, USPS, overnight courier, email, and fax transmission, Monday through Friday except on state or nationally recognized bank holidays. Purchase Orders not received by 3:00 p.m. Eastern Time are considered to be received on the following Page 1 business day. Supplier will confirm or reject Purchase Orders within [***] of receipt of the Purchase Order. Orders not rejected in writing within such time will be deemed confirmed and accepted by Supplier, unless the supplier notifies PNC verbally about rejection within 1 hour of reciept. Each Purchase Order issued by PNC or its TPMs and accepted by Supplier shall be governed by the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Additional terms included in acknowledgments, standard terms and conditions, or any other documents or communications exchanged by the Parties in connection with the sale or purchase of any Ingredients shall be void and of no force or effect. The Parties may only modify, add to or amend any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement by a writing signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause requires rejection of purchase orders 'in writing,' whereas the modification permits 'verbal' rejection within one hour. This creates an inconsistency regarding the acceptable method of rejection, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation, directly contradicting the original requirement of written rejection. This directly adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties,' causing an 'in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.'

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Supplier represents and warrants that at the time and date of delivery, the Ingredients will comply with all specifications ("Specifications"), a copy of which will be attached to the relevant Master Purchase Commitment or Purchase Order accordingly. A Specification may be updated from time to time by PNC in its sole discretion, provided PNC provides Supplier with reasonable prior notice on any updates ("Change Notification"). Within [***] from receipt of the Change Notification, Supplier will either: (1) accept the Specification change at the current price and terms; or (2) submit to PNC a proposal ("Proposal") setting forth the conditions of acceptance that may include a change in price and/or other terms, including documentation to support same. Within [***] the Parties will discuss the Proposal in good faith and exercise their best efforts to agree on the appropriate adjustment if any. PNC will not issue any Purchase Orders, nor be required to issue any Purchase Orders to Supplier until PNC and Supplier have agreed on required Ingredient Specifications and any associated price and/or term adjustment. In the event the Parties fail to agree on required Ingredient Specifications or price and/or term adjustments despite their best good faith efforts, neither Party will have any further obligation with regard to purchase or supply of those Ingredients under any Master Purchase Commitments except that PNC shall take and pay for [***] of Ingredient inventory manufactured according to the then-current Specification.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Supplier represents and warrants that at the time and date of delivery, the Ingredients will comply with all specifications ("Specifications"), a copy of which will be attached to the relevant Master Purchase Commitment or Purchase Order accordingly. A Specification may be updated from time to time by PNC in its sole discretion, provided PNC provides Supplier with reasonable prior notice on any updates ("Change Notification"). Within [***] from receipt of the Change Notification, Supplier will either: (1) accept the Specification change at the current price and terms; or (2) submit to PNC a proposal ("Proposal") setting forth the conditions of acceptance that may include a change in price and/or other terms, including documentation to support same. Within [***] the Parties will discuss the Proposal in good faith and exercise their best efforts to agree on the appropriate adjustment if any. PNC will issue Purchase Orders to Supplier based on the specifications that PNC deems fit and not bound by the agreement with the Supplier regarding ingredient specifications . In the event the Parties fail to agree on required Ingredient Specifications or price and/or term adjustments despite their best good faith efforts, neither Party will have any further obligation with regard to purchase or supply of those Ingredients under any Master Purchase Commitments except that PNC shall take and pay for [***] of Ingredient inventory manufactured according to the then-current Specification.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'PNC will issue Purchase Orders to Supplier based on the specifications that PNC deems fit and not bound by the agreement with the Supplier regarding ingredient specifications' creates an in-text contradiction. It conflicts with the previous sentences stating that PNC and Supplier must agree on Ingredient Specifications before Purchase Orders can be issued. This undermines the supplier's ability to negotiate ingredient specifications, and creates uncertainty in which specifications are being reffered to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Supplier represents and warrants that at the time and date of delivery, the Ingredients will comply with all specifications ("Specifications"), a copy of which will be attached to the relevant Master Purchase Commitment or Purchase Order accordingly. A Specification may be updated from time to time by PNC in its sole discretion, provided PNC provides Supplier with reasonable prior notice on any updates ("Change Notification"). Within [***] from receipt of the Change Notification, Supplier will either: (1) accept the Specification change at the current price and terms; or (2) submit to PNC a proposal ("Proposal") setting forth the conditions of acceptance that may include a change in price and/or other terms, including documentation to support same. Within [***] the Parties will discuss the Proposal in good faith and exercise their best efforts to agree on the appropriate adjustment if any. PNC will issue Purchase Orders to Supplier based on the specifications that PNC deems fit and not bound by the agreement with the Supplier regarding ingredient specifications . In the event the Parties fail to agree on required Ingredient Specifications or price and/or term adjustments despite their best good faith efforts, neither Party will have any further obligation with regard to purchase or supply of those Ingredients under any Master Purchase Commitments except that PNC shall take and pay for [***] of Ingredient inventory manufactured according to the then-current Specification.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stipulates that PNC and the Supplier must agree on ingredient specifications before Purchase Orders are issued. The changed text states that 'PNC will issue Purchase Orders to Supplier based on the specifications that PNC deems fit and not bound by the agreement with the Supplier regarding ingredient specifications'. This new clause directly contradicts the earlier agreement on specifications, undermining the supplier's negotiation power and creating uncertainty about which specifications govern the Purchase Orders. This is a clear inconsistency as defined by the provided Perturbation Definitions.
